title: Vienna Cod. Hebr. 120, fol. 12a — 14th-century manuscript with 1849 ownership inscription (CC-BY-SA photo)
Modern Hebrew Baseline Dataset
description: Modern Hebrew handwriting dataset from 18 contemporary individuals used as an algorithmic baseline for ostraca research.

rights.evidence_text: Figshare dataset page lists CC BY 4.0 (confirmed via Figshare API: license.name='CC BY 4.0', license.url='https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/'). However, all 3,960 images are individual character-level crops (45x45 to 438x438 pixels per letter), not full-page handwriting scans. The current entry schema requires page-level scan units; character crops do not fit the data model.

ingest.access_notes: Verify Figshare license and whether scans are full pages, lines, or other image units.
ingest.agent_notes: CC-BY-4.0 verified. Dataset is 18-writer Hebrew alphabet character recognition dataset (TAU Modern Hebrew Baseline). Zip contains Writer_N/Alphabet/NN_LetterName/*.bmp files. Useful for character-level OCR training but not page-level HTR. No entries created; status set to needs_review pending schema decision.
ingest.blocked_reason: Out of scope for page-level HTR dataset: all 3,960 images are individual Hebrew character crops (45x45 to 438x438 px), not full-page or page-fragment handwriting scans. Dataset is a character-recognition benchmark (18 writers x 22 letters x ~10 samples). Recommended as a source for character-level datasets such as HeOCR/hletterscript.

evidence.quote: released under a highly permissive CC BY 4.0 license
Modern Hebrew dataset; 18 modern individuals; CC BY 4.0

title: NYPL Digital Collections — Hebrew Manuscripts, Ketubbot, and Letters
description: Hebrew Illuminated Manuscripts, historical Ketubbot (handwritten marriage contracts), and early modern letters. Out-of-copyright materials are completely free for any use including commercial.

evidence.quote: NYPL has a policy of making all of its out-of-copyright digital materials completely free for any use, including commercial, with no permission required.

title: Katz Center for Advanced Judaic Studies — Hebrew Manuscripts (OPenn)
description: Hundreds of digitized handwritten Hebrew manuscripts, codices, and historical documents. All OPenn content is CC0 1.0 Universal.

rights.evidence_text: OPenn platform policy: all content released under CC0 1.0 Universal (Public Domain Dedication). Source: docs/sources/gemini_summary_2.md.

ingest.access_notes: Bulk rsync or FTP access (openn.library.upenn.edu). Need to identify specific Judaica sub-collections with Hebrew handwriting in scope, then filter by date range.
ingest.agent_notes: High-value lead: CC0 license, bulk access, and OPenn is explicitly designed for computational use.

evidence.quote: All of it is released under a CC0 1.0 Universal (Public Domain Dedication). You can pull entire directories of high-res TIFFs/JPEGs and XML metadata via Rsync or direct FTP.

title: Category: Handwritten Hebrew letters
description: Commons category lead for freely licensed or public-domain handwritten Hebrew letter images and related media.

rights.evidence_text: Per-file verification: 2 qualifying files ingested. File:Delacroix letter.png uses PD-old-100; File:Solitreo contract.jpg uses PD-Art|PD-old-70. Most other category files are SVG teaching samples, character-level crops, or CC-BY-SA 3.0 (excluded). Mixed rights overall; ingested items are all PDM-1.0.

ingest.access_notes: Use MediaWiki API and file pages; exclude SVG teaching samples unless the dataset explicitly wants vector handwriting examples.
ingest.agent_notes: Ingested 2 qualifying handwritten scans from Category:Handwritten_Hebrew_letters and subcategory Category:Solitreo_script (under Category:Hebrew_handwriting_scripts). Most files in the main category were excluded: SVG teaching samples, tiny character crops (<55px), group photographs, and CC-BY-SA 3.0 licensed files. Files with {{Wrong license}} template were excluded. Two public-domain Solitreo script documents qualified.

evidence.quote: community-moderated repository containing original photographic scans and SVG reproductions
files generally mandated to be freely usable media
